evidence: Python parser rejects `'output':': 0}` — the stray colon between `':` and `: 0` is a SyntaxError; ran `python -c "import ast; ast.parse(open('graphify/skill-trae.md').read())"` against the embedded code block
description: Stray colon in dict literal at --cluster-only block produces SyntaxError when the trae variant of the skill is invoked with --cluster-only
suggested_fix: Change `'output':': 0}` to `'output': 0}` (remove the stray `:': ` and keep the value `0`)

evidence: grep on the file confirms two different conventions in the same skill: Step 1 writes `graphify-out/.graphify_python` (line ~80); Step 3A subagent prompts read `$(cat .graphify_python)` (no `graphify-out/` prefix). The bash subshell will fail with `cat: .graphify_python: No such file or directory` when run from a clean working directory
description: Step 1 writes the cached Python interpreter path to `graphify-out/.graphify_python`, but Step 3A reads it as `.graphify_python` without the `graphify-out/` prefix. The same path drift is present in skill-aider, skill-claw, skill-codex, skill-copilot, skill-droid, skill-kiro, skill-opencode, skill-trae
suggested_fix: Unify all references to use `graphify-out/.graphify_python` in both write and read paths; or change Step 1 to write `.graphify_python` at the top level

evidence: The merge block contains `for chunk_json in []:` — a loop iterating over a literal empty list, with a `# PASTE each subagent response here` comment immediately above. The loop never executes, so the resulting `merged` dict has the structure but no entities/relationships. The user gets a graph with zero nodes after the merge step
description: VSCode variant's merge step at line 153-155 is a non-functional placeholder requiring manual editing — the literal empty list silently produces a no-op merge
suggested_fix: Replace the `for chunk_json in []:` loop with a file-glob-based collection (e.g., `for path in glob.glob('graphify-out/extract_*.json'):`) so the merge runs automatically without requiring the user to paste subagent responses inline

description: No `<example>` blocks in skill body — Claude has no concrete I/O to anchor on
suggested_fix: Add 2+ <example> blocks showing concrete input (a sample folder) and the resulting graph output

description: Frontmatter has `name` and `description` but no `version` field; same issue across all 11 variants

Skill is at `graphify/skill.md` rather than the canonical `skills/graphify/SKILL.md`. Note: graphify is a multi-tool skill targeting 11 different AI assistants, not exclusively Claude Code, so the non-canonical path may be intentional
suggested_fix: If Claude Code compatibility matters, add a `skills/graphify/SKILL.md` symlink or the canonical layout alongside the existing files

description: skill-copilot.md and skill-kiro.md are functionally identical to each other and to skill.md; neither has a unique Step B2 divergence — both can be deleted in favor of skill.md
suggested_fix: Delete one of skill-copilot.md or skill-kiro.md; consolidate the other with skill.md or document its platform-specific reason for existing

description: Five variants (aider, claw, opencode, copilot, kiro) are missing features that landed in canonical skill.md after the variants were forked: GitHub URL clone (Step 0), uv interpreter detection, --whisper-model, --obsidian-dir, --wiki, Node ID format spec, YouTube URL type
suggested_fix: Sync the 5 stale variants with skill.md's current feature set, or extract a shared base and tool-specific override sections

description: _clone_repo GitHub URL regex matches `github.com.evil.com/x/y` — the regex `[:/]` allows a colon and `(?:\.git)?$` anchors only the suffix not the hostname
suggested_fix: Replace regex check with `url.startswith("https://github.com/")` followed by `validate_url(url)` from security.py

description: --out / --dir arguments accept arbitrary writable paths (e.g. /etc, ~/.ssh) with no containment check; the same is true for ingest.py:1356
suggested_fix: Canonicalize the path and reject if it escapes a configured base directory; document the policy

description: _BLOCKED_HOSTS covers metadata.google.com and metadata.google.internal but not the AWS/Azure/GCP alternates; the IP-based check catches 169.254.169.254 directly so this is defense-in-depth gap not a bypass
suggested_fix: Add metadata.aws.internal, metadata.azure.com, and the IMDSv2 token endpoints to _BLOCKED_HOSTS

description: All 20+ dependencies in pyproject.toml are unpinned (no upper bound, several have no lower bound); no lockfile is committed (uv.lock, poetry.lock absent)
suggested_fix: Pin dependencies to known-good ranges; commit a lockfile (`uv lock` or `pip-compile`)
